shortforms.MS: Market Structure — The overall trend and key swing points on a chart
shortforms.MSS: Market Structure Shift — Change from bullish to bearish (or vice versa)
shortforms.BMS: Break in Market Structure — Price breaks a key structural level
shortforms.BOS: Break of Structure — Price breaking a key swing high/low (SMC term, not original ICT)
shortforms.CHoCH: Change of Character — Confirmed shift in market structure (SMC term, not original ICT)
shortforms.HH: Higher High — Each successive peak is higher than the last (uptrend)
shortforms.HL: Higher Low — Each successive trough is higher than the last (uptrend)
shortforms.LH: Lower High — Each successive peak is lower than the last (downtrend)
shortforms.LL: Lower Low — Each successive trough is lower than the last (downtrend)
shortforms.BSL: Buy Side Liquidity — Stops above highs, targeted by sell programs
shortforms.SSL: Sell Side Liquidity — Stops below lows, targeted by buy programs
shortforms.ERL: External Range Liquidity — Stops outside established range extremes
shortforms.IRL: Internal Range Liquidity — Liquidity within the current dealing range
shortforms.EQH: Equal Highs — Two or more swing highs at the same level, liquidity target
shortforms.EQL: Equal Lows — Two or more swing lows at the same level, liquidity target
shortforms.PDH: Previous Day High — Yesterday's highest price, often a liquidity level
shortforms.PDL: Previous Day Low — Yesterday's lowest price, often a liquidity level
shortforms.PWH: Previous Week High — Last week's highest price
shortforms.PWL: Previous Week Low — Last week's lowest price
shortforms.PMH: Previous Month High — Last month's highest price
shortforms.PML: Previous Month Low — Last month's lowest price
shortforms.BS: Buy Stops — Buy orders triggered above current price (stop losses)
shortforms.SS: Sell Stops — Sell orders triggered below current price (stop losses)
shortforms.LS: Liquidity Sweep — Price briefly moves into liquidity before reversing
shortforms.DOL: Draw on Liquidity — Price probing toward a liquidity pool before reacting
shortforms.FVG: Fair Value Gap — 3-candle imbalance pattern created by aggressive price movement
shortforms.IFVG: Inverse Fair Value Gap — FVG in the opposite direction, used for reversal entries
shortforms.BISI: Buy Side Imbalance Sell Side Inefficiency — FVG where buying inefficiency remains
shortforms.SIBI: Sell Side Imbalance Buy Side Inefficiency — FVG where selling inefficiency remains
shortforms.VI: Volume Imbalance — Abnormal volume indicating aggressive buying/selling
shortforms.CE: Consequent Encroachment — 50% retracement level of an FVG or imbalance
shortforms.OB: Order Block — A consolidation zone where institutional orders sit
shortforms.BB: Breaker Block — An order block that has been broken and now acts as flipped support/resistance
shortforms.MB: Mitigation Block — An order block partly mitigated by price returning to it
shortforms.RB: Rejection Block — An order block where price strongly rejected on first touch
shortforms.PB: Propulsion Block — A strong order block that propelled price through multiple levels
shortforms.PD Array: Price Delivery Array — Set of levels where price is expected to react
shortforms.OTE: Optimal Trade Entry — Entry zone at 61.8-79% retracement of a move
shortforms.EQ: Equilibrium — Midpoint of a range (50% level), acts as magnet for price
shortforms.OTE Zone: Optimal Trade Entry Zone — 62%-79% retracement region for entries
shortforms.M50: Mean Threshold 50% — The 50% retracement level, midpoint of a range
shortforms.PD: Premium / Discount — Above equilibrium (premium/sell) vs below (discount/buy)
shortforms.IPDA: Interbank Price Delivery Algorithm — ICT's model of how institutional price moves
shortforms.AMD: Accumulation, Manipulation, Distribution — The three-phase market cycle
shortforms.MMSM: Market Maker Sell Model — Institutional sell-side liquidity algorithm
shortforms.MMBM: Market Maker Buy Model — Institutional buy-side liquidity algorithm
shortforms.MM: Market Maker — Major institutions moving price to access liquidity
shortforms.HTF: Higher Time Frame — Chart timeframes above your trading timeframe
shortforms.LTF: Lower Time Frame — Chart timeframes below your trading timeframe
shortforms.MTF: Multiple Time Frame — Analyzing across multiple chart timeframes for confluences
shortforms.AZ: Asian Range — Price range during the Asian trading session
shortforms.KZ: Kill Zone — Specific time window when ICT expects institutional moves
shortforms.LO: London Open — The opening of the London session (key ICT timing)
shortforms.NYO: New York Open — The opening of the NY session, often after London
shortforms.LC: London Close — The close of the London session, often overlapping with NY
shortforms.ADR: Average Daily Range — The average daily price movement range from recent days
shortforms.ODR: Opening Range — Price range established at the beginning of a session
shortforms.DR: Dealing Range — Price range where institutional orders are being built
shortforms.BPR: Balanced Price Range — Range where buy/sell orders are balanced
shortforms.0.50: Equilibrium (50%) — The midpoint retracement level, often acted on
shortforms.0.62: OTE Beginning (62%) — Start of the Optimal Trade Entry zone
shortforms.0.705: Optimal Entry (70.5%) — ICT's preferred specific entry level in OTE zone
shortforms.0.79: Deep Discount/Premium (79%) — The deepest retracement before a reversal
shortforms.SB: Silver Bullet — Specific hour window (10am-11am or 2pm-3pm NY) for entries
shortforms.FTA: Fair Value Gap + Time Alignment — FVG aligned with specific session timing
shortforms.CRT: Candle Range Theory — Using candle bodies and wicks to determine next move
shortforms.CRDB: Consolidation, Raid, Displacement, Balance — Four-phase market cycle model
shortforms.NWOG: New Week Opening Gap — Gap between previous week close and this week open
shortforms.NDOG: New Day Opening Gap — Gap between previous day close and today open
shortforms.NWOB: New Week Opening Balance — Opening range of the new week
shortforms.NQOB: New Quarter Opening Balance — Opening range of the new quarter
shortforms.DOP: Daily Open Price — The opening price of the current daily candle
shortforms.PO3: Power of 3 — Accumulation, Manipulation, Distribution market cycle
shortforms.CISD: Change In State of Delivery — Shift in how price is being delivered (fast vs slow)
shortforms.Displacement: Displacement — Strong impulsive candle beyond the recent range
shortforms.Retracement: Retracement — A pullback against the current trend direction
shortforms.Expansion: Expansion — Strong directional move after consolidation
shortforms.Raid: Raid — A liquidity sweep into a pool of stops before reversing
shortforms.Repricing: Repricing — Fast displacement into a new price discovery area
shortforms.Rebalancing: Rebalancing — Price returning to fill inefficiencies (FVGs, OBs)
shortforms.CPI: Consumer Price Index — Key inflation report affecting all markets
shortforms.PPI: Producer Price Index — Wholesale inflation gauge
shortforms.NFP: Non-Farm Payrolls — Monthly US employment report (high impact)
shortforms.FOMC: Federal Open Market Committee — US Fed rate decision (high impact)
shortforms.COT: Commitment of Traders — Weekly report showing positions of commercial & retail traders
shortforms.SMT: Smart Money Technique / Synchronicity — Divergence between correlated assets
shortforms.SMT Div: SMT Divergence — Price difference between two correlated instruments
shortforms.SMC: Smart Money Concepts — The broader trading community term for ICT-style trading
shortforms.Judas Swing: Judas Swing — A brief false move to trap traders before the real move
shortforms.IB: Initial Balance — The first hour(s) range, used to project the day
shortforms.LVN: Low Volume Node — Price level with minimal historical trading activity
shortforms.HVN: High Volume Node — Price level with heavy historical trading activity
shortforms.RTH: Regular Trading Hours — Standard market session (9:30am-4pm ET)
shortforms.ETH: Electronic Trading Hours — Extended hours, includes overnight trading
shortforms.YY: Year — Usually refers to ICT Mentorship year (e.g. YY 2022, YY 2023)
